content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/NetworkInterfaceModal/NetworkInterfaceModal.scss

Apply ALL of the following 4 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 3
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-expandable-section-toggle]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle

Code context:
```
    2    &__advanced {
    3      .pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text {
    4        font-weight: var(--pf-v5-global--FontWeight--bold);

```

### Fix 2
Line: 3
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-expandable-section-toggle-text]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text

Code context:
```
    2    &__advanced {
    3      .pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text {
    4        font-weight: var(--pf-v5-global--FontWeight--bold);

```

### Fix 3
Line: 3
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-expandable-section-toggle]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle

Code context:
```
    2    &__advanced {
    3      .pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text {
    4        font-weight: var(--pf-v5-global--FontWeight--bold);

```

### Fix 4
Line: 3
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-expandable-section-toggle-text]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text

Code context:
```
    2    &__advanced {
    3      .pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text {
    4        font-weight: var(--pf-v5-global--FontWeight--bold);

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/NetworkInterfaceModal/NetworkInterfaceModal.scss
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current content.

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1 (Line 3 - `pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle`)
The rule says `pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle` was removed. Looking at the current file, line 3 has `pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` — so it seems a previous pass already changed the `pf-v5-` prefix to `pf-v6-`. But the rule says this class doesn't exist in v6 either. The original code context in the fix shows `.pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` — this fix is about the `__toggle` part (the parent element class), but the actual selector in the file is `__toggle-text`. This fix references a class that doesn't literally appear in the file as a standalone selector — it's about the `__toggle-text` selector which contains `__toggle` as a substring. This fix doesn't directly apply to its own separate line.

### Fix 2 (Line 3 - `pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text`)
The rule says `pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` was removed and has no v6 equivalent. The file currently shows `pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` — a previous pass swapped the prefix but the class doesn't exist in v6 either. This entire CSS rule block targeting this class should be removed.

### Fix 3 (Line 3 - same as Fix 1)
Same class, same issue. The class was removed, not renamed.

### Fix 4 (Line 3 - same as Fix 2)
Same class `pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text`, same issue. No v6 equivalent exists.

**Conclusion:** All four fixes target the same CSS rule block on line 3. The selector `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` (previously `.pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text`) references a class that was fully removed in PF v6 with no replacement. The entire rule block using this selector should be removed. Since this is the only content inside `&__advanced`, and `&__advanced` becomes empty, I should also clean up the empty `&__advanced` block. And if the whole `.NetworkInterfaceModal` block becomes empty, I should clean that up too — but let me check if it would be empty.

After removing the inner rule, `&__advanced` becomes empty, and `.NetworkInterfaceModal` becomes empty. I'll leave the file with just the empty top-level block to preserve the file structure (the file itself still exists for potential future use).

Actually, the cleanest approach is to leave the file with just the top-level selector and the `&__advanced` block empty, or remove the inner content entirely. Let me produce a minimal clean file:

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (`sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-expandable-section-toggle`): **Applied.** The selector `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` (previously `.pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text`, prefix-swapped by a prior pass) contains the removed `__toggle` BEM element. The entire rule block was removed since the class has no v6 equivalent.

2. **Fix 2** (`sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-expandable-section-toggle-text`): **Applied.** The selector `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` references a class that was fully removed in PF v6 with no replacement. The rule block targeting this class (lines 3–5) was removed, including the font-weight declaration inside it. The CSS variable `--pf-t--global--font--weight--heading--bold` (already migrated from `--pf-v5-global--FontWeight--bold` in a prior pass) was also removed as part of the dead block.

3. **Fix 3** (`semver-css-class-removed-c-expandable-section-toggle`): **Applied.** Same class as Fix 1 — the entire rule block was removed.

4. **Fix 4** (`semver-css-class-removed-c-expandable-section-toggle-text`): **Applied.** Same class as Fix 2 — the entire rule block was removed.

**Summary:** All 4 fixes targeted the same CSS rule block (`.pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text { font-weight: ... }` inside `&__advanced`). A previous pass had already swapped `pf-v5-` → `pf-v6-` and migrated the CSS variable, but the class itself was fully removed in PF v6 (not just renamed). The entire inner rule block was removed. The `&__advanced` block is left empty as it may still serve as a scoping selector for future styles.

1. **Fix 1** (`sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-expandable-section-toggle`): **Applied.** The selector `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` (previously `.pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text`, prefix-swapped by a prior pass) contains the removed `__toggle` BEM element. The entire rule block was removed since the class has no v6 equivalent.

2. **Fix 2** (`sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-expandable-section-toggle-text`): **Applied.** The selector `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` references a class that was fully removed in PF v6 with no replacement. The rule block targeting this class (lines 3–5) was removed, including the font-weight declaration inside it. The CSS variable `--pf-t--global--font--weight--heading--bold` (already migrated from `--pf-v5-global--FontWeight--bold` in a prior pass) was also removed as part of the dead block.

3. **Fix 3** (`semver-css-class-removed-c-expandable-section-toggle`): **Applied.** Same class as Fix 1 — the entire rule block was removed.

4. **Fix 4** (`semver-css-class-removed-c-expandable-section-toggle-text`): **Applied.** Same class as Fix 2 — the entire rule block was removed.

**Summary:** All 4 fixes targeted the same CSS rule block (`.pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text { font-weight: ... }` inside `&__advanced`). A previous pass had already swapped `pf-v5-` → `pf-v6-` and migrated the CSS variable, but the class itself was fully removed in PF v6 (not just renamed). The entire inner rule block was removed. The `&__advanced` block is left empty as it may still serve as a scoping selector for future styles.
